title: Question: Problems with CD using Cywin
body:
---


Author Name: **Luis Fernando  Enriquez-Contreras**
Original Redmine Message: 2232 from https://www.veripool.org



---

Hello, I am new to to Verilator and Cygwin. I have manage successfully install Cygwin, and tried to install Verilator using the tgz file. However, when I use the standard commands posted on the verilator website, I get these errors 

```
 unsetenv VERILATOR_ROOT  # For csh; ignore error if on bash
-bash: unsetenv: command not found

 cd verilator*
-bash: cd: too many arguments

./configure
-bash: ./configure: No such file or directory

 make
-bash: make: command not found

sudo make install
-bash: sudo: command not found

```

I am using Windows 10, and I am in Downloads folder in Cygwin. 
Is there a different code for installation in Cygwin. I am second year Electrical Engineering student(mostlly experienced with electromagnetics), but a complete noob with Cygwin, so I am not to familiar with the terminology. Also, once Verilator is installed do I still run it with cygwin? Thank You all so much
code_blocks:
unsetenv VERILATOR_ROOT  # For csh; ignore error if on bash
-bash: unsetenv: command not found

 cd verilator*
-bash: cd: too many arguments

./configure

sudo make install
